summary.title: TicTacDoh Function-Level Threat Model
summary.description: Function-level data flow diagram for the local Python desktop application TicTacDoh. Runtime logic is implemented mainly in tictactoe.py, using tkinter for the GUI, SQLite for local persistence, JSON save files under savegames/, and trusted in-memory runtime state.

diagrams.description: Function-level DFD for TicTacDoh as a local desktop application. No network or cloud components are included.
diagrams.title: TicTacDoh Function-Level DFD

data.name: User Input to Application Logic
data.description: Boundary crossed when usernames, passwords, move selections, opponent credentials, and game IDs enter trusted application logic from GUI input.

data.name: Application Logic to Local Persistence
data.description: Boundary crossed when the application reads or writes SQLite and JSON save data on the local filesystem.

text.text: Local Persistence to Trusted Runtime State

data.name: Local Persistence to Trusted Runtime State
data.description: Boundary crossed when JSON save data or database-derived state influences active in-memory session and gameplay state.

data.name: Local User / Player
data.description: Human user interacting with the local tkinter application through dialogs, buttons, and board clicks.

data.name: Bootstrap application
data.description: __main__ -> tk.Tk() -> TicTacToe.__init__(). Creates the root window, initialises paths and state, then invokes startup routines.

data.name: Initialise persistent storage
data.description: _init_db() -> _init_savegame_folder(). Creates or opens tictactoe.db and ensures savegames/ exists.

data.name: Create account
data.description: show_create_account() -> create_account() -> _hash_password(). Captures username and password and inserts a new user record.

data.name: Authenticate primary user
data.description: show_login() -> authenticate_user(). Verifies username and password against the users table and sets current_user on success.

data.name: Authenticate opponent and start game
data.description: show_opponent_dialog() -> authenticate_user() -> play_game(). Verifies the second player and initialises a fresh game.

data.name: Render and manage board state
data.description: display_game_board() -> on_button_click() -> make_move() -> is_winner() / is_board_full() / switch_player(). Renders the board, accepts moves, updates the board, and handles turns.

data.name: Save current game state
data.description: on_save_game() -> save_game(). Serialises board state, players, current turn, and timestamp to savegames/<game_id>.json.

data.name: Load saved game state
data.description: show_load_game_dialog() -> load_game(). Reads savegames/<game_id>.json and restores board, player_x, player_o, current_player, and game_id into active state.

data.name: Record match result and update statistics
data.description: end_game(). Inserts a games row and updates users.wins and users.losses based on winner or tie.

data.name: Retrieve and display scoreboard
data.description: get_scoreboard() -> show_scoreboard(). Queries usernames, wins, and losses and displays the scoreboard.

data.name: In-memory Runtime State
data.description: Trusted in-memory state: current_user, opponent, board, current_player, game_state, game_id.

data.name: tictactoe.db
data.description: SQLite database storing users(username, password_hash, wins, losses, created_at) and games(id, player_x, player_o, winner, created_at).

data.name: savegames/
data.description: Local directory containing JSON save files created during gameplay.

data.name: savegames/<game_id>.json
data.description: JSON save file storing game_id, board, player_x, player_o, current_player, and timestamp.

data.name: startup calls
data.description: __init__() invokes the storage initialisation routines.

data.name: startup state
data.description: Initial board, game_state, and path configuration stored in memory during bootstrap.

data.name: create/open DB schema
data.description: _init_db() creates or opens the users and games tables in tictactoe.db.

data.name: create directory
data.description: _init_savegame_folder() creates savegames/ if it does not already exist.

data.name: username + password
data.description: Credentials entered through show_create_account() dialogs.

data.name: insert username + password_hash
data.description: create_account() stores a username and MD5-derived password_hash in the users table.

data.name: insert result
data.description: SQLite returns success or duplicate-user failure to create_account().

data.name: username + password
data.description: Credentials entered through show_login() dialogs.

data.name: credential query
data.description: authenticate_user() queries the users table by username and password_hash.

data.name: auth result
data.description: SQLite returns a matching username row or no result to authenticate_user().

data.name: set current_user
data.description: Successful primary login writes current_user into trusted runtime state.

data.name: opponent credentials
data.description: Opponent username and password entered through show_opponent_dialog().

data.name: opponent credential query
data.description: authenticate_user() verifies the opponent against the users table.

data.name: auth result
data.description: SQLite returns a matching username row or no result to authenticate_user() for the opponent flow.

label.text: set opponent + reset board + set game_id

data.name: set opponent + reset board + set game_id
data.description: play_game() initialises board, current_player, game_id, and opponent in runtime state.

data.name: start game board
data.description: After opponent authentication succeeds, play_game() transitions into board rendering.

data.name: board position / move
data.description: Board button clicks flow into on_button_click() and make_move().

data.name: board + players + current turn
data.description: display_game_board() reads runtime state to render the board and determine whose turn it is.

data.name: updated board / current_player
data.description: make_move() and switch_player() update board state and the current turn in memory.

data.name: winner / tie
data.description: on_button_click() calls end_game() with the winner username or None for a tie.

data.name: save action
data.description: The Save Game button triggers on_save_game() and save_game().

data.name: board + players + current turn
data.description: save_game() reads board, current_user, opponent, and current_player from trusted runtime state.

data.name: target save directory
data.description: save_game() writes into the savegames/ directory.

data.name: write save JSON
data.description: save_game() writes game_id, board, player_x, player_o, current_player, and timestamp to savegames/<game_id>.json.

data.name: new game_id
data.description: save_game() updates the in-memory game_id before writing the save file.

data.name: game_id
data.description: The user enters a game ID through show_load_game_dialog().

data.name: lookup save path
data.description: load_game() resolves the requested save file path under savegames/.

data.name: read save file by game_id
data.description: load_game() opens savegames/<game_id>.json and reads JSON from disk.

data.name: saved game data
data.description: The JSON save file returns board, player_x, player_o, current_player, and timestamp to load_game().

data.name: restore board + identities + turn
data.description: load_game() restores board, current_user, opponent, current_player, and game_id into active runtime state.

data.name: display loaded game
data.description: After load_game() succeeds, show_load_game_dialog() transitions into display_game_board().

data.name: winner / tie + player identities
data.description: end_game() inserts a game record and updates wins and losses using current_user, opponent, and winner.

data.name: scoreboard request
data.description: The user selects View Scoreboard from the main menu.

data.name: scoreboard query
data.description: get_scoreboard() queries username, wins, and losses ordered by wins descending.

data.name: scoreboard response
data.description: SQLite returns usernames, wins, and losses for display in show_scoreboard().
